notes: Node 2.32 makes live worker-count scaling reliable. The scaler used to track a counter to decide how many threads to add; after rapid up/down changes plus node restarts that counter drifted and left the node under-threaded (e.g. running 1 worker while set to 4) until a manual restart. It now counts the actual LIVE worker threads each pass and spawns whichever are missing, so the node always converges to the configured count on its own — no more restarts needed to apply a worker-count change. Update both nodes and restart once to pick it up. (The Health-check CPU/GPU steppers are server-side file-check controls and don't affect transcode throughput; the Nodes Overview table shows the GLOBAL default, while the per-node value on the worker card is your override.)
